body.body: Design and implement trail as first-class DB object. A trail = temporal sequence of node activations with timestamps and decay. Required: (1) trails table: trail_id, started_at, context (user turn / NE run / habit eval); (2) trail_steps table: trail_id, seq, node_id, activation_weight, timestamp; (3) write trail on every cortex search/traversal; (4) trail inspection API: recent trails through node X, hot paths, gradient (rising vs fading); (5) trail-based edge strengthening: frequently walked paths get stronger edges; (6) trail renderer for matrix debugger + Igor self-inspection. Unlocks: wg_cooccur replacement (edges from trail proximity not corpus stats), free cosine compare via graph topology, Igor explaining own reasoning, matrix debugger. See memory: project_trails_and_gradients.md. Design conversation required before implementation — L-size.
body.notes: Three-part fix: (1) Named migration tails_v2_trail_id_sequence_pos — stops silent data loss from missing columns in live DB. (2) trail_metadata table: trail_id, started_at, purpose, trigger_description, turn_id, session_id, parent_trail_id, outcome_type, node_count. (3) tails.twm_obs_id nullable FK to twm_observations — makes TWM influence on memory graph observable. Trail = trace of a TWM slot propagating through the graph. Igor design doc: design_docs/trails_gap_analysis_2026-03-21.md. Needs Akien approval before worker.
body.title: Trail infrastructure — first-class activation trail primitive
